title: Inspect repo docs/skills alignment for note-lead short-teaser policy after runtime exemption. Focus only on these files in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public: skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md, docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md, docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md. Report exact lines or wording...
summary_text: Short answer: the slash-function approach is **technically valid in zsh**, but integration is only **partial**. - In `codex-orchestrator.zsh`, the new handlers are function-based: - `/vote() { _orch_run_vote_prompt "$@" }` - `/v() { _orch_run_v_prompt "$@" }` - That means `/vote` and `/v` work only when: - the user runs `zsh`, - `scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh` is sourced (typically via `~/.zshrc` snippet). - `codex exec /vote ...` is already handled by `_orch_try_exec_slash_prompt`, and this path is tested. What is currently covered: - `test-vote-launchers.sh`: - validates `vote` and `v` **binary launchers** call `codex-prompt-launch`. - `test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh`: - validates `codex exec -C ... /vote ...` dispatches to `prompt-launch vote`. - `test-install-kernel-launchers.sh`: - validates install/check of launcher scripts and snippet insertion, but not runtime slash-function dispatch behavior. What is still missing (important gaps): 1. Direct slash-function runtime coverage - No test asserts `/vote ...` or `/v ...` actually dispatches when snippet is sourced. - This is the main gap for the new approach. 2. Plain `codex /vote ...` behavior - ` _orch_try_exec_slash_prompt` is gated by `args[1] == exec`, so `codex /vote ...` is not routed by this path. - It falls through to existing `kernel`/`codex` fallback behavior instead. - There is currently no test pinning this expected behavior (or future behavior if changed). 3. Slash-token boundary/collision behavior - No regression checks for cases like `/votefoo` vs `/vfoo` token parsing and whether current prefix matching is intentional. Minimal regression tests to add (without touching files now): - In `tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh`: - Add a zsh block that: - `source .../codex-orchestrator.zsh` - `whence -f /vote` and `whence -f /v` are true - `/vote MARKER=abc` writes `prompt-launch vote MARKER=abc` - `/v MARKER=abc` writes `prompt-launch v MARKER=abc` - Add a block (if you want `codex /vote` to be supported) asserting it routes to `prompt-launch vote`. - Or, if that is intentionally unsupported, add a test asserting the current fallback (`raw-codex`/`kernel`) to lock contract. - Optionally add one negative/ambiguous token test (`/votefoo` and `/voo`) to lock the intended matching behavior. So: sound in zsh, but not yet fully covered by tests for the new slash-function path.
